name: Confidence Routing
short_description: Threshold-based decision logic that routes each utterance to accept, correct, clarify, or fallback based on ASR confidence.
detailed_description: Covers tasks that design and evaluate policies for acting on STT output with varying reliability. A routing policy maps confidence scores (and optionally entity-level scores) to one of four outcomes: accept the transcript, apply post-correction, ask the user for clarification, or hand off to a fallback path. The key objectives are keeping the wrong-confident-action rate below 2% and the clarification rate below 5% on the gold-92 benchmark without sacrificing throughput.
